article_title: Tenstorrent Launches TT-QuietBox 2, the First RISC-V AI Workstation to Run 120-Billion-Parameter Models on a Desktop

content_preview.title: Tenstorrent Launches TT-QuietBox 2, the First RISC-V AI Workstation to Run 120-Billion-Parameter Models on a Desktop
content_preview.summary: Jim Keller's Tenstorrent unveils a $9,999 liquid-cooled workstation with four Blackhole ASICs, 480 Tensix cores, and a fully open-source software stack, shipping globally in Q2 2026.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

On March 11, 2026, Tenstorrent announced the TT-QuietBox 2, a liquid-cooled desktop AI workstation built entirely on RISC-V architecture. Starting at $9,999, the machine packs four of the company's Blackhole application-specific integrated circuits (ASICs), delivering 2,654 teraflops at BlockFP8 precision with 128 GB of GDDR6 memory and 256 GB of DDR5 system memory. The system can run models up to 120 billion parameters locally, ships with an entirely open-source software stack, and...

source_verification.https://wccftech.com/tenstorrent-tt-quie: Fully confirmed: $9,999 price, four Blackhole ASICs, 480 Tensix cores, 2,654 TFLOPS at BlockFP8, 128 GB GDDR6, 256 GB DDR5, liquid cooling, 120V outlet, GPT-OSS 120B support, Llama 3.1 70B at 476.5 tok/s, Boltz-2 in 49 seconds, TT-Forge/TT-Metalium/TT-LLK/TT-Studio stack, Q2 2026 shipping, Jim Keller quote about developer control.
source_verification.https://www.theregister.com/2025/11/27/t: Confirmed: QuietBox 1 at $11,999, 16-core AMD EPYC Siena 8124P, four Blackhole P150 accelerators, 76 of 140 Tensix cores idle due to kernel optimization issues, scattered documentation. Review described it as 'trapped in a software blackhole.'
source_verification.https://riscv.org/blog/embedded-world-20: Confirmed: Embedded World 2026 March 10-12 in Nuremberg, production-ready RISC-V hardware from SiFive and Andes Technology, AndesCore D25F powering Amazfit watches with 1M+ units deployed.
source_verification.https://qualcomm.com/news/releases/2025/: Page loaded with JavaScript only — no article content rendered. Title confirms Qualcomm acquisition of Ventana Micro Systems for RISC-V CPU expertise. Details in article (Veyron V2, 32 cores, 3.85 GHz, RVA23) could not be independently verified from this fetch but are consistent with known reporting.
source_verification.https://www.tomshardware.com/news/tensto: Page did not render article content. Metadata confirms title and author (Anton Shilov). This is a 2023 background source providing roadmap context, not critical to core QuietBox 2 claims.

editor_notes.factual_accuracy: All core hardware specifications and performance claims verified against WCCFTech. The Register review from November 2025 confirms QuietBox 1 details and software maturity issues accurately described in article. RISC-V ecosystem context from Embedded World 2026 confirmed. Two secondary sources (Qualcomm, Tom's Hardware) did not render full content but provide background context only.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Balanced and appropriately skeptical. The article clearly flags unverified claims (independent benchmarks needed, Tensix core utilization unknown) and honestly acknowledges the QuietBox 1's software shortcomings. No sensationalism despite the impressive spec sheet.
editor_notes.originality: Excellent synthesis combining product announcement, predecessor review, and RISC-V ecosystem context. The comparison between QuietBox 1 and QuietBox 2 adds genuine editorial value. The Analysis section thoughtfully addresses the chicken-and-egg problem identified by The Register.
concerns: Two secondary sources (Qualcomm press release, Tom's Hardware roadmap) did not render article content for full verification, though neither contains claims critical to the core story
recommendations: Article is well-constructed with appropriate caveats about unverified performance claims
editor_notes.overall_assessment: Strong news article with thorough hardware specifications, honest acknowledgment of predecessor shortcomings, and valuable RISC-V ecosystem context. Primary sources fully verified. Approved.
